methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: X-Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (IPO), post-money $2B+

data.narrative:
Deep Fission, a nuclear energy company at IPO stage founded by Liz Muller and Rich Muller, has raised $80M to date. The valuation universe spans 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-stage nuclear energy firms including X-Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector contains only one same-stage IPO-ready peer, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—falling below the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. Expanding to all eight nuclear energy private companies would introduce stage and valuation heterogeneity that would distort multiples rather than illuminate them. The three public comparables exist but span different sub-segments within nuclear (advanced reactors, fuel cycles, reactor design), limiting direct multiple transfer.

This analysis reveals a fundamental constraint: Deep Fission occupies a narrow cohort with insufficient direct peers for triangulated valuation. Revenue or EBITDA multiples from the broader peer set risk misleading conclusions given capital intensity, development stage variance, and regulatory timelines. Any valuation conclusion requires either (1) reliance on the three public comps with explicit stage-adjustment reserves, (2) DCF-based methodology tied to project-specific deployment assumptions, or (3) acknowledgment that the range is anchored primarily to $80M capital raised rather than market comparables. Key uncertainties include reactor commercialization timing, regulatory approval path, and relative technology risk versus peers.
